records.ety: [OE. enqueste, OF. enqueste, F. enqute, LL. inquesta, for inquisita, fr. L. inquisitus, p. p. of inquirere. See Inquire.]

quotes: The laborious and vexatious inquest that the soul must make after science.

defs: Judicial inquiry; official examination, esp. before a jury; as, a coroner's inquest in case of a sudden death.

A body of men assembled under authority of law to inquire into any matter, civil or criminal, particularly any case of violent or sudden death; a jury, particularly a coroner's jury. The grand jury is sometimes called the grand inquest. See under Grand.
The finding of the jury upon such inquiry.
